evals.prompt: Add metrics and quantification to these resume bullets: 1) 'Managed a team of engineers' 2) 'Reduced customer complaints' 3) 'Led a successful product launch' 4) 'Improved the deployment process' 5) 'Grew the user base'
evals.expected_output: Quantified versions of all 5 bullets, or probing questions to extract the numbers needed
expectations: Output asks targeted questions to extract specific numbers for each bullet
Output provides example quantified versions showing what the final bullet could look like
Output offers multiple quantification options per bullet (time saved, % improvement, $ impact, scale)
Output explains the logic of why each metric strengthens the bullet
Output produces final quantified bullets once numbers are provided or estimated

evals.prompt: I know I reduced customer support tickets but I don't have exact numbers. I know I launched 3 features in one year, managed roughly 8-10 engineers, and our team's products serve about 500K users. Help me add credible, honest quantification to my resume without fabricating numbers.
evals.expected_output: Honestly quantified resume content using the approximations provided with appropriate hedging language
expectations: Output uses the provided approximations (8-10 engineers, ~500K users, 3 features)
Output recommends appropriate hedging language ('approximately', 'up to', 'across team of 8-10')
Output does not fabricate specific percentages the candidate cannot verify
Output suggests ways to estimate the support ticket reduction from available data
